farmDetail.noAssessmentBody: Water-stress readings appear after the first satellite data arrives — usually within a day of adding your field boundary, crop, and planting date.

trafficLight.redMessage: Your crop is under water stress now. Irrigation is recommended.
trafficLight.yellowMessageDays: Approaching stress — about {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} of water left.
trafficLight.yellowMessage: Approaching stress — keep an eye on this field.
trafficLight.greenMessageDays: No stress expected for roughly {days} days at the current pace.
trafficLight.unknownMessage: Not enough data yet to assess stress.
trafficLight.staleBanner: Satellite data is delayed — this reading may be out of date

savings.title: Water savings
savings.empty: No savings data yet. Add your typical weekly water use and log your irrigations — savings appear after the first full week.
savings.energyLine: {kwh} kWh of pumping energy · {co2} kg CO₂ avoided

irrigationLog.title: Log irrigation
irrigationLog.modeQuestion: How do you want to log it?

glossary.VWC: Volumetric water content — how much of the soil volume is water right now.
glossary.FieldCapacity: The most water the soil can hold after draining. Think of it as the soil's 'full tank' line.
glossary.MAD: Management Allowed Depletion — how much water the crop can use up before stress starts. Crossing it means it's time to irrigate.
glossary.ET: Evapotranspiration — water leaving the field through soil evaporation plus crop use, measured by satellite.
glossary.PAW: Plant Available Water — water still in the root zone that the crop can actually reach.
glossary.RAW: Readily Available Water — the share of PAW the crop can use without slowing down. Running past it causes stress.
glossary.WaterDeficit: How far the root zone is below its full line. Bigger deficit = closer to stress.

setup.title: Finish setting up this farm
setup.baselineWhy: We compare this baseline to what you actually use to calculate your water savings.
setup.baselineGallons: Gallons per week (before using this app)
setup.pumpWhy: Pump details turn gallons saved into energy and cost savings.

equity.title: A little about you (optional)
equity.why: These questions help us report program impact for grant funding that keeps this tool free for small farms. Answering is completely voluntary and never affects your service.
equity.sociallyDisadvantaged: Do you identify as a socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher?
equity.beginningFarmer: Have you been farming for 10 years or less?

savingsPage.title: Water savings — {year}
savingsPage.empty: No weekly savings yet — they appear after your first full week of logged irrigation.
savingsPage.chartCaption: Bars: gallons saved per week (red = used more than baseline). Line: cumulative savings.

impact.title: Our impact
impact.subtitle: Real water savings from small farms using satellite-based stress alerts.
impact.farmsMonitored: {count, plural, one {# farm monitored} other {# farms monitored}}
impact.privacyNote: Aggregated across all participating farms — no individual farm data is shown.
impact.comingSoonBody: We publish aggregate results once enough farms are participating to protect individual privacy.

press.title: Impact & Press
press.intro: AquaAlert alerts small farmers before their crops hit water stress, and turns avoided over-irrigation into reportable water, energy, and carbon savings.
press.whatBody: A traffic-light alert (green / yellow / red) tells a farmer when a field is approaching water stress, days in advance — no agronomist, no sensors, no spreadsheets. Farmers log irrigations with one tap, and the savings are computed automatically.
press.dataBody: Our public dashboard reports cohort-level water saved, pumping energy saved, and CO₂ avoided, refreshed nightly.
press.methodEt: Field-level evapotranspiration from OpenET satellite data (updated daily, ~5-7 day lag).
press.methodSim: Crop water balance simulated with AquaCrop, the UN FAO crop model.
press.methodSavings: Savings = farmer-reported baseline use minus logged irrigation; energy from pump lift at 55% pumping-plant efficiency; CO₂ via EPA eGRID (CAMX).
press.methodPrivacy: Public numbers are aggregates only, suppressed below 3 farms; voluntary demographic data is never published.
press.contactBody: For interviews, data, or partnership inquiries: moisesvaldez075@gmail.com

createFarm.title: Add a farm
createFarm.subtitle: Just a name gets you started — crop, planting date, and soil unlock stress alerts.
createFarm.name: Farm name
createFarm.fieldBoundaryHint: Zoom to your field, then use the polygon tool to trace its edges. Click your first point again to finish. Satellite water data needs this boundary.
